reasoning: The event category 'Battles' in ACLED requires "violent interactions between two organized armed groups at a particular time and location". The Faroe Islands is an extremely peaceful territory governed by Denmark, with no active local armed groups, no history of internal violent conflict, and essentially no baseline for ACLED battles (the reference rate is exactly 0.000). While there are minor geopolitical tensions regarding Russian fishing vessels and the strategic location of the islands, these do not involve organized armed groups engaging in violence, nor is there any credible indication that an open battle will erupt there within 2024-2025. Given the requirements for the 'resolution positive' outcome—occurring in the exact 30-day window before each specific resolution date—the probability is exceedingly low, practically at the floor allowed by the constraints, with minimal but slight increases for longer time horizons just to account for unforeseeable "black swan" events or extremely unlikely miscoding events in the ACLED dataset.
